questions.question: Which of the following statements represents the law of excluded middle?
questions.explanation: The law of excluded middle states that a statement is either true or false, with no middle ground.

questions.question: What is the logical equivalent of (A ∧ B) ∨ C?
questions.explanation: Using the distributive law, (A ∧ B) ∨ C is logically equivalent to A ∨ (B ∧ C).

questions.question: Which of the following statements is an example of the law of identity?
questions.explanation: The law of identity states that a statement is logically equivalent to itself, i.e., A ≡ A.

questions.question: What is the logical equivalent of A ∧ (B ∨ C)?
questions.explanation: Using the distributive law, A ∧ (B ∨ C) is logically equivalent to (A ∧ B) ∨ (A ∧ C).

questions.question: Which of the following statements represents the law of contradiction?
questions.explanation: The law of contradiction states that a statement cannot be both true and false at the same time, i.e., A ≡ ¬A.
